PURPOSE
=======

The purpose of this policy is to ensure that appropriate information security controls are applied to all information assets. All organizational assets are properly maintained and managed in a way that their security is not compromised.

SCOPE
=====

This policy applies to all systems, people and processes that constitute the **YOTTABYTE** information systems, including all employees and other third parties who have access to **YOTTABYTE**’s information systems.

POLICY STATEMENTS
=================

Asset Lifecycle
---------------

*   All purchases of assets must adhere to the **YOTTABYTE** Procurement process. 
*   All information technology related assets must be authorised by and acquired through the IT Department after authorization of the CEO.
*   This includes all types of assets, as noted in the Asset Inventory section of this policy. 
*   This applies regardless of the value of the asset (including free/no-cost assets). 
*   Assets must be configured securely according to the **YOTTABYTE** configuration management standard. 
*   All assets must be returned upon termination of employment or contract. 
*   Assets that are being disposed of, donated, or transferred to a third party by **YOTTABYTE** must be wiped using methods that eliminate the ability to recover data.

Asset Inventory
---------------

*   **YOTTABYTE** information and technology assets, whether on- or off-premises (including cloud), must be identified, inventoried, maintained, and documented. 
*   ISO is responsible for maintaining and updating the Information Asset Register/ Asset inventory process. All in-scope Information assets have owners who ensure their protection and accountability (accountability of information assets).
*   All updates to the Information Asset Register must be reviewed by ISO.
*   For every in-scope asset only the owner is the one responsible for editing an information asset, all the others, as per the information asset classification, can only view/read the information assets.
*   The following assets should be considered in the inventory process inclusive of **YOTTABYTE** assets in the cloud: 
    *   Authorized assets 
    *   Software, including Software as a Service (SaaS) 
    *   Databases and other information stores 
    *   Code Repositories

Asset Register
--------------

*   Information Asset Register is maintained and updated by **YOTTABYTE**. The template is designed for this purpose which contains following fields:
    *   Information asset
    *   Information asset type/category (i.e., hard copy or soft copy)
    *   Storage/Physical Location (folder path / department, section name)
    *   Asset Owner
    *   Information asset Order level (in terms of confidentiality, integrity, and availability)
    *   Information asset Value (high, medium, or low)
    *   Information asset classification

Asset/Data Classification
-------------------------

*   All data assets must be classified in terms of legal requirements, value to the organization, their criticality to the organization and sensitivity if they were to be disclosed by an unauthorised party.  Data are classified into one of the following categories: 
    *   **Restricted**: Critical data whose existence or content is only known to a small group of individuals.  Disclosure of this information would harm or impede ability to achieve corporate objectives. 
    *   **Confidential**: Data that requires an elevated level of protection (for example, encryption) to satisfy regulatory requirements or prevent their unintended disclosure.  Data is disclosed only to authorised individuals on a need-to-know basis.
    *   **Internal Use**: Data that is used to conduct the normal business operations of YOTTABYTE.  Normal levels of information handling (per the Acceptable Use Policy) are applied. The information is intended for internal use only. 
    *   **Public**: Information that **YOTTABYTE** has published for general or public consumption, or publicly known information that **YOTTABYTE** has received from other organizations. 
*   Assets must be labelled and handled based on the appropriate level of data classification. 
*   All hard copy or paper-based documents must be labelled (marked) in accordance with the information classification scheme published within this standard.  
*   The appropriate confidentiality labelling must be visible on the front page, and every subsequent page of a document. It must be uniform and leave no doubt about the level of confidentiality.  
*   Unlabeled documents must be treated as Restricted until they are properly classified. 

Removable Media Handling
------------------------

*   Removable media must also be managed by the same data classification and handling procedures. 
*   Removable media must be physically protected against unauthorised access, misuse and tampering while in use and in transit and must be disposed of securely using appropriate procedures. 
*   Media containing information must include logical protections against unauthorized access, misuse, or corruption. 

Acceptable Use of Assets
------------------------

*   An Acceptable Use Policy shall be in place to detail the user obligations of **YOTTABYE** IT Assets.

Return of Assets
----------------

*   On termination/ resignation/ retirement/ end of contract terms of an employee, all information assets issued to the concerned person shall be recovered prior to settlement of dues and departure from the organization.
*   Respective business unit heads shall ensure knowledge transfer for any employee or external party before leaving the organization.
*   HR team shall document and ensure the return of all **YOTTABYTE’s** issued assets prior to the individual’s departure.
*   In case the assets are not returned, HR team shall raise an incident to the Incident Management Team.
*   During the notice period, the organization shall control unauthorized copying of relevant information (e.g.: intellectual property) by terminated employees and contractors.

Labelling of Information
------------------------

*   Information assets (storage media, software media, paper documents, information stored in computer systems) shall be labelled physically or electronically in accordance with their Asset classification scheme.
*   Physical labelling of documents and removable media shall include appropriate security classifications in accordance with Asset Classification as defined earlier.
*   All classified assets covered shall be appropriately labeled to ensure that they are given adequate protection.
*   All physical assets (Desktops, Laptops, Digital Screen Wall, Printers etc.) shall have a unique label as per a standard convention.
*   Sensitivity Criteria for labelling and handling shall be based on:
    *   Impact on Confidentiality refers to the value of the impact on confidentiality if information is disclosed to third parties.
    *   The impact on the operations and data integrity of YOTTABYTE, if the information was tampered with, illegally changed, or updated, is referred to as the impact on integrity.
    *   Impact on Availability refers to the impact on the operations and security posture of **YOTTABYTE** if the asset is not available when it is needed.
*   Information and information processing facilities shall be labelled using the following scheme.
    *   **Public** – Information being public that cannot harm the organization in any way. Such information comes from public sources or is provided by **YOTTABYTE** to the public.
    *   **Confidential** – Private Information type owned by **YOTTABYTE** with sole rights.
    *   **Internal Use** – Information assets that do not need special security controls. The information is not in the public domain.
    *   **Restricted to know** – Confidential information is a very sensitive type of data. This information is only shared with those who have a "Need to Know" for it.

ANNUAL REVIEW
=============

The ISMS-related documents shall be reviewed once every year, or whenever the ISMS is updated. Internal Audit for the Information Security Management System based on the ISO standard(s) covered in the scope shall be carried out at least once a year and a report shall be submitted to the ISF (Information Security Forum). All the processes and procedures shall be reviewed at least annually to ensure compliance with the current changes and practices.

RELATED REFERENCE(S)
====================

**Related Standard(s)**

**Detailed Reference(s)**

ISO/IEC 27001:2022

Annex A.5.9, A.5.10, A.5.11, A.5.12, A.5.13
